name: Chipotle Sauce#4
recipeInstructions:
1. Puree the can of chipotles in a blender or food processor.
2. Strain the mixture through a sieve to remove skins and seeds.
3. Heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat.
4. Saute the garlic cloves for 4-5 minutes until golden brown and softened.
5. In a bowl of a food processor, combine the chipotle puree, sauteed garlic, whole egg, and egg yolk.
6. Process to a smooth purée, slowly adding the remaining olive oil in a thin stream until the sauce becomes slightly thicker than heavy cream but not as thick as mayonnaise.
7. Add salt to taste and refrigerate until ready to use.
8. Use as a dipping sauce with shrimp, scallops, fish, potato salad, or cold grilled chicken.
9. The sauce will keep for 3-5 days in the refrigerator. Make sure the eggs used are very fresh.
